revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五色素麺]]
[[File:Coloured noodles (3757201181).jpg|thumb|250px|right|五色素麺]] '''Goshiki Sōmen''' (五色素麺) is one of the {{ill|Regional cuisine|en|Regional cuisine|qid=Q94951|lt=local cuisines|1=Regional cuisine}} handed down in {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}} and {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City|1=Matsuyama}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|Iyo-bushi|ja|伊予節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo-bushi|qq=}}, it is sung as one of the "famous specialties and landmarks of Matsuyama in Iyo," known as "Goshiki Sōmen, renowned for its sound"{{R|案内}}. It is also mentioned in the publication "Shokoku Meibutsu-kan" (『{{ill|Shokoku Meibutsu-kan|ja|諸國名物鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokoku Meibutsu-kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』) released during the {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|qid=Q1325001|lt=Genroku era|1=Genroku}}, and has long been recognized as a specialty{{R|案内}}.
== Origin == It is said that the invention was devised by '''Nagato-ya''' of {{ill|松山|en|松山|qid=Q1216599|lt=Matsuyama|1=松山}} {{R|案内|47c}}.
'''Nagato-ya''' was established by Ichizaemon, who accompanied {{ill|Matsudaira Sadayuki|ja|松平定行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Sadayuki|1=Matsudaira Sadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10729440}} when he relocated to the {{ill|Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|en|Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|qid=Q1042087|lt=Iyo Matsuyama Domain|1=Iyo-Matsuyama Domain}} in the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kanei era|1=Kan'ei}} 12 ({{ill|1635|en|1635|qid=Q6879|lt=1635|1=1635}}){{R|案内|47c}}. In the {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|qid=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō era|1=Kyōhō}} 7 ({{ill|1722|en|1722|qid=Q6918|lt=1722|1=1722}}), the daughter of the eighth Ichizaemon, while visiting {{ill|Tsubaki Shrine|ja|椿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Shrine|1=Tsubaki Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11541039}} ({{ill|Iyo Mamehiko no Mikoto Shrine|ja|伊豫豆比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Mamehiko no Mikoto Shrine|1=Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11380620}}), observed five-colored thread entangled in her geta and suggested to her father that color be added to somen noodles—an incident said to have sparked the creation of this dish{{R|47c}}.
Initially, the noodles were sold under the name "Nagato-ya Somen," but later the name was changed to "Goshiki Sōmen"{{R|案内}}. Eventually, it became known even to the shogun and {{ill|court nobles|ja|殿上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court nobles|1=court nobles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10369708}}, and during the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kanei era|1=Kan'ei}} and {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|qid=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō era|1=Kyōhō}}, it was presented at the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|禁裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Palace|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禁中|1=Imperial Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11591117}}{{R|案内}}. It is said that at that time an appraisal was made, "as beautiful as a gem of interwoven threads, displaying five colors"{{R|案内}}.
{{ill|Chikamatsu Monzaemon|en|Chikamatsu Monzaemon|qid=Q324986|lt=Chikamatsu Monzaemon|1=Chikamatsu Monzaemon}}, who was on friendly terms with the prominent Matsuyama merchant Gotō Kozawemon, was also gifted Goshiki Sōmen, and a letter of thanks to Gotō praising the dish still exists{{R|47c}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書|page=455|title=愛媛県史 原始-近代|volume=第16巻|year=1982|publisher={{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}}}</ref>.
{{ill|Masaoka Shiki|en|Masaoka Shiki|qid=Q315152|lt=Masaoka Shiki|1=Masaoka Shiki}} left a verse in the 26th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治26|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504459|1=1893}}—"Oh, the somen of Fumizuki, with five-colored threads"{{R|47c}}—and his disciple {{ill|Takashi Nagatsuka|en|Takashi Nagatsuka|qid=Q554810|lt=Chōzuka-bushi|1=Takashi Nagatsuka}} recorded in his diary that he had tasted Goshiki Sōmen during a visit to {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en|Dōgo Onsen|qid=Q1037748|lt=Dogo Onsen|1=Dōgo Onsen}}{{R|47c}}. In {{ill|Heisei 29|ja|平成29|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei 29|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504461|1=2017}}, as part of the "Masaoka Shiki and {{ill|Natsume Sōseki|en|Natsume Sōseki|qid=Q180903|lt=Natsume Sōseki|1=Natsume Sōseki}} 150th Birthday Commemoration Project," a commemorative version of Goshiki Sōmen was released by Goshiki Sōmen Co., Ltd. Morikawa<ref>{{Cite web|和書|website={{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City|1=Matsuyama}}|url=https://www.city.matsuyama.ehime.jp/shisei/machizukuri/kotoba/shikisoseki150.html|title=子規・漱石生誕150th year 記念の取り組み（終了しました）|date=2018-05-16|accessdate=2022-12-16}}</ref>.
== Sources == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="案内">{{Cite book|和書|page=40|url=https://www.google.co.jp/books/edition/%E6%9D%BE%E5%B1%B1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/Wj9QYeJ1HAMC?hl=ja&gbpv=1&dq=%22%E4%BA%94%E8%89%B2%E7%B4%A0%E9%BA%BA%22+-wikipedia&pg=PT3&printsec=frontcover|title=松山案内 松山道後名所図絵 : 松山・道後大観 : 松山遊覧の栞 : 絵はかき|year=1931|publisher={{ill|Matsuyama City Hall|ja|松山市役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama City Hall|1=Matsuyama City Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11529806}}}}</ref> <ref name="47c">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.47club.jp/38M-000055nsa/?mkwid=L6mdVBJs-dc_pcrid__pkw_%E4%BA%94%E8%89%B2%E3%81%9D%E3%81%86%E3%82%81%E3%82%93%E6%A3%AE%E5%B7%9D_pmt_b|website=47club|title=五色そうめん株式会社|accessdate=2022-12-16}}</ref> }}
=== Related Articles === * {{ill|Goshiki Soba|ja|五色そば|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshiki Soba|1=Goshiki Soba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11372612}}
== External Links == * [http://www.iyokannet.jp/front/gourmet/detail/gourmet_id/54 愛媛の「これだけは外せない」グルメ特集] - {{ill|Iyokan Net|ja|いよ観ネット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyokan Net|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Official Ehime Prefecture Tourism Site) * [http://www.i-manabi.jp/system/regionals/regionals/ecode:1/7/view/1274 データベース『えひめの記憶』・愛媛の技と匠（平成9th year 度）] - {{ill|Ehime Lifelong Learning Center|ja|愛媛県生涯学習センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Lifelong Learning Center|1=Ehime Lifelong Learning Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11493582}}
{{food-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|五色素麺|version=97870918|comment=Imported full ja history}}
